anchor.snippet: If the state space is the integers or natural numbers, then the stochastic process is called a discrete or integer-valued stochastic process

Random walks are stochastic processes that are usually defined as sums of iid random variables or random vectors
A classic example of a random walk is known as the simple random walk
this random walk is called a symmetric random walk
The Wiener process is a stochastic process with stationary and independent increments that are normally distributed based on the size of the increments
then the resulting stochastic process is said to have drift
a sample path of a Wiener process is continuous everywhere but nowhere differentiable
which is the subject of Donsker's theorem or invariance principle, also known as the functional central limit theorem
It can be defined as a counting process, which is a stochastic process that represents the random number of points or events up to some time

this stochastic process is also called the stationary Poisson process
the resulting process is called an inhomogeneous or nonhomogeneous Poisson process

The finite-dimensional distributions of a stochastic process satisfy two mathematical conditions known as consistency conditions
Stationarity is a mathematical property that a stochastic process has when all the random variables of that stochastic process are identically distributed

A filtration is an increasing sequence of sigma-algebras
A modification of a stochastic process is another stochastic process
the Kolmogorov continuity theorem says that there exists a modification of this process that has continuous sample paths with probability one

Separability is a property of a stochastic process based on its index set in relation to the probability measure

that have the Markov property, which means the next value of the Markov process depends on the current value, but it is conditionally independent of the previous values

annotations.label: Brownian motion and Poisson as Markov processes

anchor.snippet: are both examples of Markov processes
A Markov chain is a type of Markov process that has either discrete state space or discrete index set
which results in collections of random variables known as Markov random fields

A martingale is a discrete-time or continuous-time stochastic process with the property that, at every instant, given the current value and all the past values of the process, the conditional expectation of every future value is equal to the current value
